```crystal
require "array"
require "algorithm"

# Helper function to check if array B is a cyclic rotation of array A.
# Assumes A and B have the same length.
def is_cyclic_rotation(a : Array(Int64), b : Array(Int64)) : Bool
  k = a.size
  if k == 0
    return true
  end

  # Concatenate A with itself: A' = A + A
  a_double = a.dup
  a_double.concat(a)

  # Check if B is a contiguous subarray of A' starting at some index j in [0, k-1]
  # We iterate through all possible starting points j in the first copy of A.
  for j in 0...k
    match = true
    for i in 0..<k
      # Compare B[i] with A'[j + i]
      if b[i] != a_double[j + i]
        match = false
        break
      end
    end
    if match
      return true
    end
  end

  return false
end

def solve(nums : Array(Int64)) : Int64
  n = nums.size
  
  # 1. Calculate S, the sorted version of nums.
  s = nums.dup
  s.sort!

  # 2. Find all divisors k of n.
  divisors = []
  for k in 1...Math.sqrt(n).to_i
    if n % k == 0
      divisors.push(k.to_int64)
      if k * k != n
        divisors.push(n / k.to_int64)
      end
    end
  end
  divisors.sort!

  total_sum = 0 : Int64

  # 3. Check each divisor k.
  for k_int64 in divisors
    k = k_int64.to_int
    is_sortable = true

    if k == 1
      # Special case k=1: Requires nums to already be sorted.
      if nums != s
        is_sortable = false
      end
    else
      # General case k > 1: Check if every block S_i is a cyclic rotation of C_i.
      for i in 0...(n / k)
        start_idx = i * k
        
        # C_i: Original chunk from nums
        c_i = nums[start_idx...start_idx + k]
        
        # S_i: Target chunk from sorted array s
        s_i = s[start_idx...start_idx + k]

        # Since s_i is derived from s (a permutation of nums), and c_i is a segment of nums,
        # the multiset equality is guaranteed if the overall array is a permutation,
        # which it is. We only need to check the rotation property.
        
        if !is_cyclic_rotation(c_i, s_i)
          is_sortable = false
          break
        end
      end
    end

    if is_sortable
      total_sum += k_int64
    end
  end

  return total_sum
end
```

require "array"

class Solver
    def solve(nums : Array(Int64)) : Int64
        n = nums.len
        
        # 1. Sort nums to get S
        s = nums.dup
        s.sort!

        # 2. Find divisors k of n
        divisors = Array(Int32).new
        (1..Math.sqrt(n.to_f).round).each do |i|
            if n % i == 0
                divisors.push(i.to_i32)
                if i * i != n.to_f.round
                    divisors.push((n / i).to_i32)
                end
            end
        end

        sum_k = 0.to_i64

        divisors.each do |k|
            k_int = k.to_i64
            is_sortable = true

            # 4. Iterate through blocks
            (0..n / k - 1).each do |i|
                start_idx = i * k
                end_idx = (i + 1) * k - 1
                
                # Extract blocks A_i and S_i
                a_i = nums[start_idx..end_idx]
                s_i = s[start_idx..end_idx]

                # iii. Content Check: A_i must be a permutation of S_i
                # Since S_i is sorted, we check if sorted(A_i) == S_i
                a_i_sorted = a_i.dup
                a_i_sorted.sort!
                
                unless a_i_sorted.eql?(s_i)
                    is_sortable = false
                    break
                end

                # v. Cyclic Shift Check
                if k > 1
                    # Check if S_i is a cyclic shift of A_i
                    
                    # O(k^2) check: Iterate through all k possible shifts p
                    shift_found = false
                    (0..k - 1).each do |p|
                        match = true
                        (0..k - 1).each do |j|
                            # Check S_i[j] == A_i[(j + p) % k]
                            if s_i[j] != a_i[(j + p) % k]
                                match = false
                                break
                            end
                        end
                        
                        if match
                            shift_found = true
                            break
                        end
                    end

                    unless shift_found
                        is_sortable = false
                        break
                    end
                # If k=1, the content check already ensures A_i == S_i, so it passes.
                end
            end

            # c. Accumulate sum
            if is_sortable
                sum_k += k_int
            end
        end

        return sum_k
    end
end

# Define the required function signature wrapping the class method call
# The provided signature uses Array(Int64) for input and Int64 for return.
def solve(nums : Array(Int64)) : Int64
    solver = Solver.new
    return solver.solve(nums)
end

def is_cyclic_permutation(A : Array(Int64), S : Array(Int64), k : Int) : Bool
    # Assumes A.len == S.len == k
    if k <= 0
        return false
    elsif k == 1
        return A[0] == S[0]
    else
        # Create A concatenated with itself: A_double
        A_double = A ++ A
        
        # Check all k possible rotations of A against S
        # We only need to check starting points j from 0 to k-1
        k.times do |j|
            is_match = true
            # Check if S matches the slice starting at j in A_double
            k.times do |i|
                if S[i] != A_double[j + i]
                    is_match = false
                    break
                end
            end
            if is_match
                return true
            end
        end
        return false
end

def is_cyclic_permutation(A : Array(Int64), S : Array(Int64), k : Int) : Bool
    # Assumes A.len == S.len == k
    if k == 0
        return false
    elsif k == 1
        return A[0] == S[0]
    else
        # Double A
        A_double = A ++ A
        
        # Check all k possible rotations
        k.times do |j|
            # Check if S matches A_double starting at j
            is_match = true
            k.times do |i|
                if S[i] != A_double[j + i]
                    is_match = false
                    break
                end
            end
            if is_match
                return true
            end
        end
        return false
end

def solve(nums : Array(Int64)) : Int64
    n = nums.len
    
    # 1. Get sorted version S
    S = nums.dup.sort
    
    total_sum = 0.0
    
    # 2. Find divisors k

        end

            total_sum += k
        end
    end
    
    total_sum.to_i # Since the return type is Int64, and k is Int, the sum should fit standard Int64
end
